title: OpenAI confirms rare GPT-5.6 file deletions in unrestricted Codex sessions
friendlyTitle: OpenAI says GPT-5.6 deleted files in a few full-access sessions
summary: OpenAI product lead Tibo Sottiaux says the company investigated a handful of cases in which GPT-5.6 unexpectedly deleted files. The reported pattern involved Codex running in Full Access Mode without sandboxing or auto review, then mistakenly deleting the home directory while trying to redefine it as temporary storage; OpenAI says it is adding guidance and harness safeguards.
friendlySummary: OpenAI says GPT-5.6 sometimes deleted a user’s home folder after making a mistake in Codex Full Access Mode. The affected sessions did not use sandboxing or automatic safety review.
whyItMatters: Anyone using coding agents on a real machine should keep sandboxing and review protections enabled, use version control and maintain tested backups. “Full access” turns an ordinary model mistake into a potentially irreversible system failure.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Do not give a coding agent more access than it needs. Keep safety checks on, use version control and make backups before the agent changes important files.
